candidates.title: Spectral Sensitivity Analysis: BSS Diagnostic + Adaptive Method Selection
candidates.summary: Develop Bucketed Spectral Sensitivity (BSS) as a theoretically grounded, seed-stable, per-test-point diagnostic of attribution reliability. Use BSS to guide adaptive IF/RepSim selection. Gated by variance decomposition showing genuine per-sample structure beyond class membership.
hypotheses: H-G1: Residual per-sample variance > 30% after class+gradient-norm control
H-D1: BSS outlier-bucket ranking seed-stable (Spearman rho > 0.5)
H-D2: BSS predicts IF reliability (rho > 0.4 with LDS)
H-F1: Adaptive Pareto-dominates uniform by > 2% LDS
H-F2: BSS fusion > naive ensemble by > 1.5% LDS
candidates.pilot_focus: Phase 0: Re-analyze Probe data for class-conditional TRV decomposition (0 GPU-hrs). Phase 1: Full-model variance decomposition on 500 CIFAR-10 test points (~5 GPU-hrs). This is the gating experiment.
key_risks: Residual per-sample variance may be < 20% (class dominates)
BSS may reduce to class detector (Papyan 2020 class-structure)
Adaptive routing may not beat uniform at equal compute (routing tax)
Full-model Hessian code engineering may exceed time budget

candidates.title: Structural Limitations of Per-Test-Point TDA Diagnostics
candidates.summary: Rigorous negative results paper demonstrating that per-test-point attribution sensitivity is dominated by class membership and gradient magnitude. ANOVA decomposition across datasets/architectures, Pareto frontier showing uniform > adaptive, stability-correctness analysis. Constructive recommendation: class-conditional method selection.
hypotheses: H-Con1: Class + gradient norm explain > 80% of TRV/BSS/disagreement variance
H-Con2: Uniform methods dominate Pareto frontier at every compute budget
H-Con3: Fixed-weight ensemble within 2% LDS of any adaptive strategy
H-Con4: Stability-correctness partial correlation < 0.1 after class control
candidates.pilot_focus: Re-analyze Probe data: class-conditional TRV means per seed. If between-class variance > 60% in all seeds, strong early signal for this direction.
key_risks: Negative results face editorial bias at top venues
Residual per-sample variance may be borderline (20-30%), complicating the narrative
Need multi-dataset validation (CIFAR-100, Tiny-ImageNet) for generalizability
candidates.weakest_assumption: That CIFAR-10 results generalize to other datasets

candidates.title: Conformal Prediction Sets for TDA Reliability
candidates.summary: Replace point-estimate attribution rankings with conformal prediction sets that have distribution-free coverage guarantees. The set size serves as a provably valid, per-model-instance reliability diagnostic. Sidesteps cross-seed problem by calibrating per model instance.
hypotheses: H-Inn3: Conformal sets have valid coverage >= 90% with CV > 0.5 across test points
H-Inn4: Conformal set size correlates with IF-RepSim disagreement (rho > 0.4)
candidates.pilot_focus: Compute TRAK-50 calibration ground truth for 300 test points, then conformal calibration. Bottleneck is ground truth computation.
key_risks: Nonconformity score for rankings is non-trivial to design
Set sizes may be uniform (no diagnostic value)
High compute cost for calibration ground truth
candidates.weakest_assumption: That conformal prediction extends cleanly to ranking spaces

meta.next_action: Run Phase 0 (zero-compute reanalysis of Probe data) + Phase 1 gating experiment (variance decomposition, ~5 GPU-hrs)
